response_text:
As of my knowledge cutoff in October 2023, there has been no official announcement or inclusion of a garbage collector in the Rust language, even in the upcoming 2024 edition. Rust is designed around the concept of memory safety without needing a garbage collector, using its unique ownership and borrowing system to manage memory.

If there are any ongoing proposals or experimental features related to garbage collection in Rust, they would likely be found in Rust's RFC (Request for Comments) process or as part of unstable features in nightly releases. However, Rust's core philosophy emphasizes minimal runtime overhead, and introducing a garbage collector would be a significant shift.

For the most up-to-date information, I recommend checking the official Rust blog, Rust RFCs, or the Rust language repository on GitHub. If there were any changes or experimental features introduced after October 2023, these sources would provide the latest details and instructions on how to use them.
